projects.title: Unit 8 Project — Statistics in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: describe the center, spread, and overall shape of your data distribution.

projects.title: Distribution Detective
projects.desc: Identify symmetric, skewed, and clustered shapes in data distributions (6.SP.2).

projects.title: Histogram Master Lab
projects.desc: Read histograms to see the shape and spread of a distribution.

projects.title: Unit 8 Graphic Novel
projects.desc: Story-driven comic exploring statistics and data distributions.

title: Shape of Data Distributions
contentObjective: I can describe the shape of a data distribution as symmetric, skewed, or having clusters and gaps.
languageObjective: I can explain my description using the words symmetric, skewed, cluster, and gap.

turnAndTalk.question: The analyst graphs player heights, 3-pointers per game, and coaches' years of experience. Why might each data set have a DIFFERENT shape when graphed?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Different real-world data sets form different distribution shapes: symmetric, skewed, or clustered.

stems.en: The shapes are different because the data ___.
stems.es: Las formas son diferentes porque los datos ___.

stems.en: A symmetric shape looks ___, while a skewed shape looks ___.
stems.es: Una forma simétrica se ve ___, mientras que una sesgada se ve ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain different data produces different shapes and can describe symmetric (balanced) versus skewed (lopsided) distributions.
turnAndTalk.extend: Predict: which of the three data sets is most likely to be skewed, and why?
extendStems: I predict ___ is most likely skewed because ___.
The values would pile up on one side since ___.

turnAndTalk.question: As you match each data description to its shape, how can you tell a symmetric distribution from a skewed one just by looking?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A symmetric distribution is balanced around the center; a skewed one has a longer tail on one side.

stems.en: A symmetric distribution has data that is ___ on both sides.
stems.es: Una distribución simétrica tiene datos ___ en ambos lados.

stems.en: A skewed distribution has a longer ___ on one side.
stems.es: Una distribución sesgada tiene una ___ más larga en un lado.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer says symmetric data mirrors around the center while skewed data has a longer tail pulling to the left or right.
turnAndTalk.extend: Compare: how do clusters and gaps in a distribution give you extra information beyond just 'symmetric' or 'skewed'?
extendStems: A cluster tells me ___, while a gap tells me ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Marathon times cluster at 3.5-4.5 hours, with elites at 2.0-2.5 and a long tail of slower runners at 5-7 hours. What is the shape, and should the reporter use the mean or median for a 'typical' time?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A skewed distribution is better summarized by the median than the mean.

stems.en: This distribution is skewed to the ___ because of the long tail of ___.
stems.es: Esta distribución está sesgada hacia la ___ por la cola larga de ___.

stems.en: The reporter should use the ___ because ___.
stems.es: El reportero debería usar la ___ porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify the right-skewed shape (long tail of slow runners) and choose the median because the slow tail would pull the mean up.
turnAndTalk.extend: Critique: the reporter wants the finish times to sound fast. Which measure might they pick, and would that be honest given the shape?
extendStems: To sound fast, the reporter might pick the ___ because ___.
That would be honest/misleading because the shape is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A data set has most values clustered between 40-60 with a few at 90-100. What is the shape of the distribution, and which measure of center is best?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A few high values create a right-skewed shape, so the median is the better center.

stems.en: The shape is skewed to the ___ because of the values at ___.
stems.es: La forma está sesgada hacia la ___ por los valores en ___.

stems.en: The best measure of center is the ___ because ___.
stems.es: La mejor medida de centro es la ___ porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students describe a right-skewed shape (cluster at 40-60, gap, then 90-100) and choose the median because the high values would inflate the mean.
turnAndTalk.extend: Generalize: state a rule connecting the SHAPE of a distribution to the best measure of center to use.
extendStems: If the shape is symmetric, the best center is the ___.
If the shape is skewed, the best center is the ___ because ___.

vocabulary.definition: Data that looks about the same on the left and right.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Datos que se ven casi iguales a la izquierda y a la derecha.
vocabulary.visual: Heights: 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 66, 64, 62, 60 — rises and falls evenly like a hill
vocabulary.cloze: A distribution where both sides look like mirror images is ___.

examples.text: Data balanced evenly around the center
examples.why: Both sides match, so it is symmetric.

examples.text: Data with a long tail to the right

vocabulary.definition: Data bunched on one side with a tail on the other.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Datos amontonados de un lado con una cola del otro.
vocabulary.visual: Skewed right: most data low (1,2,2,3,3,3,15) — tail stretches toward the high value
vocabulary.cloze: A distribution with a tail stretching out to one side is ___.

vocabulary.definition: A group of numbers that are close together.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un grupo de números que están cerca unos de otros.
vocabulary.visual: Scores 18, 19, 20, 21, 22 form a cluster around 20 — they are tightly grouped
vocabulary.cloze: A group of data values bunched close together is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A big empty space where there is no data.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un espacio grande y vacío donde no hay datos.
vocabulary.visual: Data: 5, 6, 7, 8, ___, ___, ___, 20 — the empty space from 9 to 19 is a gap
vocabulary.cloze: An interval where there are no data values is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: How the data looks: where it sits and how spread out it is.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cómo se ven los datos: dónde están y qué tan separados están.
vocabulary.visual: Looking at a dot plot or histogram, you can see if data is symmetric, skewed, clustered, or has gaps
vocabulary.cloze: The way data values are spread out across a range is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the numbers are.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están los números.
vocabulary.visual: Symmetric data with low variability: tightly packed around the center. Skewed data: spread unevenly
vocabulary.cloze: How much the values in a data set differ from one another is the ___.

launch.narrative: The league analyst is studying three different data sets from the season: (1) Player heights across all teams, (2) Number of 3-pointers per game by each player, and (3) Years of experience for all coaches. Each data set has a different shape when graphed. Your job is to describe each shape and explain what it tells us about the data.
launch.contextImage: Three different histogram shapes displayed on a sports analytics dashboard
noticePrompts: Do the three data sets look the same when graphed?
Which graph has data bunched in the middle? Which has a long tail?
Are there any gaps or clusters you can spot?
wonderPrompts: Why would different sports data have different shapes?
Does the shape tell you which measure of center to use?

visual.title: Example: 3-pointers per game (skewed right)
visual.caption: Most players make few 3-pointers; a few make many — the tail stretches RIGHT (skewed right).

conceptIntro.heading: What shape is the data?
conceptIntro.intro: The shape of a data distribution describes how the values are spread out. Data can be symmetric (the same on both sides), skewed (bunched on one side with a long tail on the other), or have clusters and gaps.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Symmetric data mirrors around the center; skewed data has a long tail, and the skew is named for the direction the tail points.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: Example 1, bar heights from left to right: 2, 5, 8, 5, 2. The bars rise to a peak in the middle and fall evenly on both sides.
Both sides look like mirror images, so this shape is symmetric.
Example 2, bar heights: 9, 6, 3, 1. The tallest bars are on the left and the bars get shorter going right.
The long tail points to the right, so this shape is skewed right. I name the skew after the tail, not the peak.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: New bar heights: 1, 3, 6, 9. Where are the tallest bars, and which way does the tail point?
Since the tail points left, is this symmetric, skewed right, or skewed left?
Now picture heights 2, 6, 6, 2. Do both sides match? What shape is that?

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Next, you will match each sports data description to its shape: symmetric, skewed right, or skewed left.
Look for the long tail to decide the direction of the skew.

explore.label: Match each sports data description with the shape of its distribution.

cards.text: Player heights: most players are around 5'8"–5'10", with equal numbers taller and shorter
3-pointers per game: most players make 0–2, a few make 3–5, and rarely anyone makes 6+
Free-throw percentage for experienced players: most shoot 70%–90%, a few shoot below 50%
100-meter dash times: most runners are around 12–13 seconds, equal numbers faster and slower
Career home runs: most players hit fewer than 100, a few superstars hit 400+
Senior athletes' reaction times: most are fast (0.2–0.3s), a few are very slow (0.6s+)

discourse.prompt: When data is skewed right, is the mean or median a better measure of center? Why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When data is skewed right, the tail pulls the ___ toward the ___. The ___ is a better measure because it is not affected by ___. For symmetric data, the mean and median are ___.

optional.stem: Display 1: A histogram of points-per-game has a few short bars stretching out to the high-scoring side, with a long tail of high values on the right. What shape is this distribution?
optional.explanation: A tail of high values stretching to the right means the distribution is skewed right. The skew is named for the tail's direction.

optional.stem: Display 2: A dot plot of starting players' heights has one clear peak in the middle, with roughly even sides falling away from that peak. What shape best describes this distribution?
choices: Symmetric with one cluster around the peak
optional.explanation: One peak in the middle with roughly even sides means the data mirrors around the center, so it is symmetric with a single cluster at the peak.

optional.instructions: Sort each described sports display into the shape feature it shows best.

items.text: Goals per game: one peak at 1 goal, an even rise and fall on both sides
Coach experience (years): most coaches at 1-5 years, a long tail out to 30+ years
Veteran kickers' field-goal %: most near 90%, a long tail down to a few near 40%
Race times: a group at 12s, nothing from 13-19s, then a group at 20s
Jersey numbers: most players bunched tightly between 20 and 29

optional.label: Display 4: Match each feature you spot in a data display to the term that names it.

pairs.term: The tallest point of the display where data piles up
An empty interval with no data values between two groups
A group of values bunched close together
A long stretch of low bars trailing off to one side

optional.stem: Display 5: A free-throw percentage histogram has most players clustered between 70% and 85%, a clear empty interval from 50% to 65%, and a few players down at 40%-50%. Which features are present, and what is the overall shape?
choices: A cluster (70-85%), a gap (50-65%), and a left tail — so skewed left
A symmetric display with no gaps
A right tail of high values — so skewed right
Two equal peaks with no cluster or gap
optional.explanation: The data clusters at the high end (70-85%), there is an empty gap (50-65%), and the few low values form a tail on the left, so the display is skewed left.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Name the Skew by Its Tail

workedExample.label: Display
workedExample.work: Analytics screen: minutes played per game. Bar heights left to right: 12, 8, 4, 2, 1.

workedExample.label: Cluster
workedExample.work: Most players are bunched on the left (low minutes).

workedExample.label: Tail
workedExample.work: The short bars trail off toward the right (high minutes).

workedExample.label: Shape claim
workedExample.work: Because the tall bars (the peak) are on the LEFT, this display is left-skewed.

optional.correctWork: This display is skewed RIGHT, not left. Skew is named for the direction the TAIL points, not where the peak sits. The peak is on the left, but the long tail of short bars stretches to the right, so it is skewed right. (A correct claim-evidence-reasoning answer: 'The distribution is skewed right because I see the tail stretching toward the high values on the right.')
hints: Ask: which way does the long tail of short bars point, not where is the tallest bar?
The peak is on the left and the tail stretches right. Skew is named for the tail, so this is skewed right.

approaching.stem: A histogram of basketball players' ages shows: most players are 22–28, with a long tail of older players up to age 40. What is the shape of this distribution?
approaching.explanation: Most data is on the left (younger ages) with a tail stretching right (older ages). This is skewed right.

approaching.stem: A data set is symmetric. What is likely true about the mean and median?
choices: The mean is much higher than the median
The median is much higher than the mean
They are always exactly the same
approaching.explanation: In a symmetric distribution, the mean and median are approximately equal because the data is balanced on both sides.

approaching.stem: Match each description with the correct distribution shape.

pairs.left: Equal amounts of data on both sides of the center
Most data on the left, tail stretches right
Most data on the right, tail stretches left
Data values grouped tightly with nothing nearby

approaching.stem: Which word describes the direction of the TAIL, not the peak?
choices: Skew — the direction of the long tail tells you the skew direction
Skew — the direction of the peak tells you the skew direction
Skew — the direction the median points
Skew — the direction of the mode
approaching.explanation: The skew direction is named after the TAIL. If the tail stretches to the right, it is skewed right, even though the peak is on the left.

approaching.instructions: Sort each histogram description into its shape.
hints: Symmetric: bars rise and fall evenly from the center. The pattern mirrors itself.
Skewed right: tallest bars on the left, trailing off to the right. Skewed left: opposite.
approaching.label: Sort each bar pattern into the correct distribution shape.

onLevel.stem: A dot plot of goals scored per game shows: 0(5 dots), 1(7 dots), 2(4 dots), 3(2 dots), 4(1 dot). What is the shape?
choices: Skewed right — most values at the low end with a tail to the right
Skewed left — most values at the high end
Uniform — all bars the same height
onLevel.explanation: The data peaks at 0–1 goals and tails off toward 4. Most data is on the left with fewer values stretching right — this is skewed right.

onLevel.label: Match each distribution shape to how its mean and median compare.

pairs.match: Mean ≈ Median — use either (mean is fine)
Mean > Median — use the median (high tail pulls the mean up)
Mean < Median — use the median (low tail pulls the mean down)

onLevel.stem: Match the data shape with the best measure of center.

pairs.left: Data with a large gap and cluster

onLevel.instructions: For each data set, decide: is it symmetric, skewed right, or skewed left?
hints: Look for outliers or tails. A high outlier creates a right skew. A low outlier creates a left skew.
If data is roughly even on both sides of the center, it is symmetric.
onLevel.label: Classify each data set by its distribution shape.

extending.title: Find the Shape Error

workedExample.label: Shape
workedExample.work: Skewed left because the tall bar is on the right side

workedExample.label: Best measure
workedExample.work: Use the mean because skewed data uses the mean

extending.correctWork: This is skewed RIGHT, not left. The cluster of data is on the left (10–11 seconds) and the tail with the extreme value (14.8) stretches to the right. Also, for skewed data the MEDIAN is the better measure, not the mean, because the mean is pulled toward the tail.
hints: The direction of the skew is the direction of the TAIL, not the peak. Where is the long tail?
The peak is on the left (10–11 seconds) and the tail stretches right to 14.8. That means skewed RIGHT.

extending.prompt: A teacher shows two dot plots of quiz scores from two different classes. Class A's data is symmetric with a cluster around 80. Class B's data is skewed right with most scores between 60–70 and a few scores near 100. Compare the classes: which has a higher median? Which has a higher mean? In which class does the mean better represent a typical score?
extending.sentenceFrame: Class A is ___ with scores clustered around ___. Class B is skewed ___ with most scores at ___. Class A likely has a ___ median. Class B's mean is pulled ___ by the high scores. The mean better represents a typical score in Class ___ because ___.

extending.instructions: Drag each real-world data set under the distribution shape it would most likely form.

categories.label: Skewed Left
items: An experienced archer's scores out of 100

hints: Symmetric: most values cluster in the middle with equal tails on both sides.
Skewed RIGHT: a few very HIGH values (e.g., superstar earnings) stretch the tail right.
Skewed LEFT: a few very LOW values (e.g., a few bad rounds) stretch the tail left.
extending.label: Sort each data set by its likely histogram shape.

optionalActivity.name: Distribution Shape Gallery Walk
optionalActivity.intro: Walk the league's analytics gallery and name the shape of each sports data display you see.

connect.scenario: A sports reporter is writing about marathon finish times. The data shows: most runners finish between 3.5–4.5 hours, with a small group of elite runners finishing in 2.0–2.5 hours, and a long tail of slower runners finishing in 5–7 hours.
connect.promptQuestion: What is the shape of this distribution? Should the reporter use the mean or median to describe a 'typical' finish time?
connect.prompt: The distribution is skewed ___ because the tail stretches toward the ___ finish times. There is a cluster around ___. The reporter should use the ___ because the ___ would be pulled toward the ___ by the extreme values.

exitTicket.stem: A data set has most values clustered between 40–60, with a few values at 90–100. What is the shape of the distribution and which measure of center is best?
exitTicket.explanation: The data clusters on the left (40–60) with a tail to the right (90–100). This is skewed right. The median is best because the mean would be pulled toward the high values.
